title: Farmhouse Hall Tree
summary: A tall entryway piece combining a storage cubby bench below with a jigsaw-shaped hook rail above — a genuine bench-plus-hooks hall tree distinct from this catalog's simpler entryway benches.
description:
This hall tree is a genuinely two-part build: a bench-height storage cubby section on plywood, topped by a tall jigsaw-shaped hook panel that rises well above seat height — combining what this catalog's other entryway benches keep as separate pieces (a bench and a wall-mounted hook rail) into one continuous unit. Build the bench storage area first from ripped plywood, following the cubby layout, then add bottom supports that transfer a sitter's weight straight down to the floor rather than through the cubby dividers alone.

**The face frame comes next, built with pocket holes and attached with finish nails and glue** — this is what gives the piece its finished furniture look rather than exposed plywood edges. Base moulding wraps the bottom of the bench section, and the plan notes a genuine option here: raise the bottom 1" and use a wider 3-1/4" moulding instead of the standard 2-1/4" if you want a chunkier base profile.

The hook panel's shape is cut from a 1x12 with a jigsaw — **clamp the two panel pieces together and sand until they match exactly**, the same matching technique this catalog uses for other paired jigsaw cuts, since a hand-shaped profile that doesn't mirror cleanly is immediately visible on a piece this prominent. The back panel goes on last, shown split into three separate boards specifically so the whole assembly can still fit through a car trunk or door if you're transporting it after building.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

Split into three boards for transport; combined width covers 48".
Cut to fit around the bench base.

steps.title: Build the bench storage area
steps.body: Build the bench box from the ripped plywood pieces (top, bottom, dividers, end panels), following the cubby layout, using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet

steps.title: Add the bottom supports
steps.body: Attach the bottom support rails under the bench so a sitter's weight transfers directly to the floor.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and attach to the bench box with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the base moulding
steps.body: Wrap the base moulding around the bottom of the bench section, mitering corners as needed.

steps.title: Build the hook panel back frame
steps.body: Build the vertical back frame for the hook panel from the 1x4 sides and 1x3 rails with pocket holes, attaching it above the bench section.

steps.title: Add the top moulding
steps.body: Attach the top trim and top cap across the top of the hook panel frame.

steps.title: Shape and attach the hook panel sides
steps.body: Mark the shaped profile on the 1x12 hook panel side pieces and cut out with a jigsaw. Clamp the two pieces together and sand until they match exactly. Attach in place.

steps.title: Attach the back and finish
steps.body: Attach the three back panel sections, then attach coat hooks. Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Farmhouse hall tree with a storage bench and coat hook panel
